gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/38)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/38)
text: سَمِعْتُ الرِّضَا ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ إِذَا أَدْخَلَ طَعَامَ سَنَتِهِ خَفَّ ظَهْرُهُ وَ اسْتَرَاحَ وَ كَانَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ وَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) لَا يَشْتَرِيَانِ عُقْدَةً حَتَّى يُحْرَزَ إِطْعَامُ سَنَتِهِمَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, form Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Al Hassan Bin Al Jaham who said,

‘I heard Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘The human being, when he saves the foodstuff for his (whole) year (expenditure), his back is lightened and he would be at rest, and Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> and Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> were not buying their ties (of marriage) until they<sup>asws</sup> had saved foodstuffs for their<sup>asws</sup> year’.
en.sarwar: 1. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Faddal from al-Hassan ibn al-Jahm who has said the following: “I heard al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, saying, ‘If one saves food for one year, his burden becomes light and he feels comfortable. Abu Ja’far and abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, would not buy anything before reserving their food for one year.”
